feed.subtitle: Latest gigs and events in Oslo, Norway
feed.title: Oslo Gig Guide

entry.content:
Venue: Revolver
Start: 2026-08-20 20:00
Tags: rock, punk, hardcore


Shadow Cave Riot

Shadow Cave Riot har omtalt seg selv som lyden av demokratiets undergang, tapte drømmer, sinnets uhelse og hverdagens drittsekker. Andre har omtalt musikken deres som veldig fengende punkinspirert rock.

Sloss: "Det er vi som er sloss."


Tickets: revolveroslo.ticketco.events
entry.summary: Shadow Cave Riot x Sloss at Revolver on 2026-08-20 20:00
entry.title: Shadow Cave Riot x Sloss (2026-08-20)

entry.content:
Venue: Vulkan Arena
Start: 2026-09-04 18:00
Tags: Bingo, DJ


Looking for the best bingo party near you? Gear up for a night out in Oslo at the bingo rave where you will experience a show as you’ve never experienced before. 

What does that include?

✈️│Win holidays along with random prizes ranging from lawnmowers to giant teddy bears! 🎶│DJs playing the biggest tracks of the of 90s +00s!  🍻│Boozy bingo 🎊│ Mountains of confetti showers!  🎤│Hilarious hosts that will have you on the floor laughing!  👯🏽‍♂️│Dance-offs!  🥳│And so much more!

Friday 4th september 2026 Vulkan Arena, Oslo

More info: https://eu.bingoloco.com/oslo

Doors open: 6:00 PM / 18H00 Show starts: 7:00 PM / 19H00 Show ends: 10:00 PM / 22H00

Seating: First come, first served. Get your squad together and arrive early to claim the best seats in the house. Each table seats 8-10, so be friendly and make room for others!

Disclaimer: May contain adult content


Tickets: eu.bingoloco.com
entry.summary: BINGO LOCO at Vulkan Arena on 2026-09-04 18:00
entry.title: BINGO LOCO (2026-09-04)

entry.content:
Venue: KAFÉ HÆRVERK
Start: 2026-10-10 19:00
Tags: Rock, Garage


The Nomads celebrated 40 years in the rock’n’roll ring during 2021. Their most recent album, “Solna” was named after the Stockholm suburb from which they sprang in 1981. The first time I talked to Nick Vahlberg was in 1983 for Sounds when that particular paper blazed the trail for the great garage rock scare of the time. He was sceptical when I suggested they had staying power. The big thing now seems to be for bands to reform in some shape or form to cash in on whatever kudos might remain. Considering this, it requires to be noted that these guys never went away. There was no game plan prior or during the recording of “Solna”. They just decided to see what would happen. Nick sees it like this. “The album took a ridiculously long time to make so it was a relief that it all turned out well in the end. Drummist, Joakim Ericson adds, “It’s been a long journey. I just played it simple and straightforward, no gambles or frills. Heavy and steady with a degree of authority was what I was going for.” And he adds, “Listening to the result, I’m happy that I took that approach.The production is great with so much going on that anything complicated wouldn’t have fitted.” Bass basher, Björn Fröberg is pivotal in the songwriting process because if it weren’t for him then it’s doubtful the others would be anywhere near as prolific. “Chips (Kiesbye) and I were already writing songs for it and ended up demo’ing 30 of those. The idea was to come up with something that was as good as Sonically Speaking or The Cold Hard Facts of Life in terms of sound. The process took a long time because Chips was busy recording as that is how he makes a living. He could only work on it when things during quiet periods. Despite the fact of how long we took, I´m very proud of what we ended up with.” Chips is also a member of the great Swedish rockin’ combo, Sator. He says, “The Nomads single-handedly started the garage rock revival in Sweden. They were the proof that you could move forwards by looking back. “Other than the Pebbles compilations, not much was known about ‘60’s garage bands and many people first heard such great songs when The Nomads covered them.” But one thing that sets the band apart from many other revival acts is that they never merely copied the sound of the originals. They added additional influences such as punk, power pop and hard rock. That special recipe is what still makes their sound unique.” Bands like The Hellacopters and Sator are direct descendents of what The Nomads created in the post-Cramps frenzy that has since fizzled into psychedelic oblivion. This earned them the moniker of “Solna’s Sonics”. These guys were more true to the Australian strain of punk rock. Taking strands of every strain and weaving it into something entirely their own. If they decided to cover something then you weren’t able to tell that it wasn’t theirs. Another jump that they have over many of their contemporaries is their chemistry. Live they’re a powerhouse, delivering what, in a perfect world, should have been hit after hit. Anyone that needs a crash course on what that statement refers to should grab a copy of the compilation “Nomadic Dementia”. This will instantly provide the proof that I’m not talking out of my hat. Hans Östlund is a mighty guitar player of Walter Lure-mungous proportions and much more but we’ll come back to that. His take on the recent activity goes like this, “The specific difference this time was obviously the lack of a studio in traditional terms. Apart from the drums and some of the guitars, it was all recorded in our rehearsal space. I must say that I actually enjoy it even more these days and even have a “hobby” punk band, Aoousch! – so it seems like I can’t get enough of playing.” The band even backed up Roky Erickson for a one-off festival outing and immediately after the show the one- time 13th Floor Elevator’s ex-wife paid Hans a very high compliment. “Directly after the show, she told me – “you’re the best guitar player Roky has had since Duane (Aslaksen)”. I guess I just said “thanks” and then I had to find a quiet place where I could let that sink in.” There are plans to follow up Solna but as yet, only one new song “Working For The Man” has been released as part of a split single on Ghost Highway Recordings with Norwegian pop sensations, The Dahlmanns. A full album may well come together in 2022 but they’re in no hurry. There’s an air of it’ll happen if it happens. Meanwhile, there are sporadic live appearances and Finland was treated to two shows during November 2021. This combo is very much a going concern. Lowdown shakin’ rock’n’roll chills just don’t come any better that The Nomads. Never did and never will. They’re as good now as they ever were. Very probably better. Lindsay Hutton, 2021 Dører: 19:00 Konsertstart: 20:00 Inngang: 400.-+avgift ID: 20 https://www.thenomads.se/ https://www.facebook.com/thenomadsofficial https://www.instagram.com/thenomadsofficial/ https://open.spotify.com/artist/1oy9v5jsSnvtNrjgr0rr8t?si=xSZGuFslR7iAZDfz6PppjQ


Tickets: tikkio.com
entry.summary: THE NOMADS #2// Kafé Hærverk at KAFÉ HÆRVERK on 2026-10-10 19:00
entry.title: THE NOMADS #2// Kafé Hærverk (2026-10-10)

entry.content:
Venue: KAFÉ HÆRVERK
Start: 2026-10-14 20:00
Tags: Jazz, Impro, experimental


Karl Bjorå er en musiker og komponist bosatt i Oslo, som er en del av en rekke samarbeid

i et stort sjangerspenn. I alt fra Emmeluth’s Amoeba til WIZRD til Martin Horntveth’s

Gouldian Finch har han bidratt med gitarspillet sitt, og komponert musikk for egne band

samt Megalodon Collective. Musikken til denne trioen er også komponert av Bjorå, og

beveger seg en plass i mellom alt han tidligere har holdt på med.

Sammen med seg i trioen har han Ingebrigt Håker Flaten på bass. Ingebrigt er en bauta i

moderne norsk jazz, og har gjort seg kjent gjennom Atomic, Young Mothers, Close Erase

og i nyere tid med Cimota og uttallige konstellasjoner over hele verden ellers.

Barndomsvennen og den ubegrensete supertrommisen Ole Mofjell er siste del av trioen.

Ole og Karl har spilt sammen siden de var 16 år, og spiller for tiden jevnt sammen i

Emmeluth’s Amoeba. Ellers kjenner man Ole fra sin trio, Three Days of Maceration,

Weejuns og en rekke ad hoc prosjekter.

Karl Bjorå - gitar Ingebrigt Håker Flaten - bass Ole Mofjell - trommer Dører: 20:00 Konsertstart: 20:30 Inngang: 250.-+avgift ID: 20


Tickets: tikkio.com
entry.summary: Karl Bjorå trio // Kafé Hærverk at KAFÉ HÆRVERK on 2026-10-14 20:00
entry.title: Karl Bjorå trio // Kafé Hærverk (2026-10-14)
